blockingIssues.description: OpenBurnBarDaemonServer constructs BurnBarMissionControlService without executionReadinessGate, so production dispatch path skips readiness checks.
MissionControlService readiness enforcement is optional when executionReadinessGate is nil, which keeps fail-open semantics instead of fail-closed defaults.
VAL-DAEMON-011 test coverage includes a nil-gate pass-through case that codifies fail-open dispatch behavior and conflicts with milestone requirements.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require explicit evidence that newly introduced runtime gates are wired into daemon/server composition roots, not only service-level seams.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature m8 review found readiness gate added to service but never injected from OpenBurnBarDaemonServer, leaving runtime behavior fail-open.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a mandatory checklist item to execute and report every feature verificationStep surface (daemon + app + extension when specified) before handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m8 feature metadata required app and extension verification commands, but worker handoff only ran daemon/core commands.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add handoff accuracy rule: claimed symbols/paths in whatWasImplemented must be verifiable in committed diff via grep or file references.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m8 handoff claimed BurnBarTransportExecutionReadinessProbe, but reviewer found no symbol match in the commit.
